mission.title: argo: Support for Azure Entra workload identity
mission.description: Support for Azure Entra workload identity . Requested by 64+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Review argo configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get configmap -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo
```
More and more cloud providers are supporting the concept of workload identity. I believe this is also already supported in argocd for GCP. It would be great if ArgoCD also implemented the token exchange for a

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support for Azure Entra workload identity
steps.description:
Workaround for the poor souls who found this through google and are stuck in the same place:

```bash
export ACR_NAME="targetacr"
# The username of the TARGET account
export USER_NAME="argocd-helm-token-$ACR_NAME"
# Creates a token
export PASSWORD=$(az acr token create -n $USER_NAME \
             
```yaml
export ACR_NAME="targetacr"
# The username of the TARGET account
export USER_NAME="argocd-helm-token-$ACR_NAME"
# Creates a token
export PASSWORD=$(az acr token create -n $USER_NAME \
                  -r $ACR_NAME \
                  --scope-map _repositories_pull \
                  --only-show-errors \
                  --query "credentials.passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
# add the repo to argo
 argocd repo add $ACR_NAME.azurecr.io/helm --username $USER_NAME --password $PASSWORD --enable-oci --type helm --name helm --project default
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support for Azure Entra workload identity " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Workaround for the poor souls who found this through google and are stuck in the same place:

```bash
export ACR_NAME="targetacr"
# The username of the TARGET account
export USER_NAME="argocd-helm-token-$ACR_NAME"
# Creates a token
export PASSWORD=$(az acr token create -n $USER_NAME \
                  -r $ACR_NAME \
                  --scope-map _repositories_pull \
codeSnippets:

# The username of the TARGET account
export USER_NAME="argocd-helm-token-$ACR_NAME"
# Creates a token
export PASSWORD=$(az acr token create -n $USER_NAME \
                  -r $ACR_NAME \
                  --scope-map _repositories_pull \
                  --only-show-errors \
                  --query "credentials.passwords[0].value" -o tsv)
# add the repo to argo
 argocd repo add $ACR_NAME.azurecr.io/helm --username $USER_NAME --password $PASSWORD --enable-oci --type helm --name helm --project default

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
